picks.rationale: Concrete, actionable data point for self-publishers: Kobo now rejects 45% of submissions and tightened moderation after an AI-book flood.
picks.excerpt: E-book platform Rakuten Kobo was forced to reject 45% of submissions to its self-publishing platform last year, after a flood of AI-generated books were uploaded, prompting tighter moderation of Kobo Writing Life.

picks.rationale: Reframes AI for publishers from defensive IP anxiety to an active growth strategy spanning creation, translation, marketing and rights.
picks.excerpt: AI is not another incremental shift: it touches every part of what publishers do, from creation and curation to production, translation, marketing and rights management. The piece argues the industry must move from IP anxiety toward an AI growth strategy.

picks.rationale: A live precedent for how prize bodies adjudicate AI-use claims: drafts, time-stamps and notes cleared accused winners, shaping author practice.
picks.excerpt: Three writers accused of using AI to create their Commonwealth Short Story Prize-winning pieces have been cleared after a review in which all five regional winners supplied drafts, time-stamped documents and notes.

picks.rationale: New product move: Gemini-built Book Insights puts a spoiler-free AI companion inside the reading experience, a fresh format for publishers to weigh.
picks.excerpt: Google Play Books has launched Book Insights, a Gemini-built reading companion that offers spoiler-free recaps and lets readers ask questions while reading select titles, raising fresh questions about AI inside the reading experience.

dropped_items.reason: Content duplicate of already-published books card 'books-beijing-fair' (Beijing International Book Fair, June 17-21 2026) covered via Publishing Perspectives on 2026-06-26. Same event, different outlet.
